data.title: The humanoid robot revolution is upon us - Nvidia CEO
data.description:
A new "iPhone moment" is being hailed by the robotics industry, with a powerful era underway for manufacturing and domestic assistance. "When, literally, humanoid robots are wandering around, is not five years away, this is a few years away," Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said this week at the GTC conference. The company, along with others, already has a lot of skin in the game, with its RTX GPUs powering bots and the firm's coming Isaac GR00T N1 serving as a generalist foundation model.

Next generation: As the staple of any sci-fi movie in the past, humanoid robots are now moving closer to reality due to advances in artificial general intelligence. It's part of what's being called "embodied AI," which harnesses AI technology inside of hardware - in this case a robot that can carry out real-world functions. Up until recently, most bots relied on code to perform their tasks, which limited their applications. The promise of AI will allow them to learn new jobs by reasoning and imitation, or eventually identifying and solving situations like a human by "generalizing across common tasks."

"We know very clearly that the world has severe shortages of human laborers, human workers," Huang continued. Humanoid robots never need to take a day off, can work around the clock, and are able to be used in multiple settings. That includes the workforce, like in factory environments or the assembly line, as well as the home, for tasks such as vacuuming, folding laundry, doing dishes, and even companionship.

In the race: One of the biggest known players is Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Optimus, which Elon Musk has called the "biggest product in history" with plans to produce up to 1M units by 2027 (could it help reignite the stock?). Another U.S.-based player is Brett Adcock's Figure AI, which is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and OpenAI. The company's bots are geared toward manual labor tasks like logistics and warehousing, and have already been deployed in BMW factories. Others out there include Atlas by Boston Dynamics and Phoenix by Sanctuary AI, as well as Chinese bots like Unitree's G1, CyberOne by Xiaomi and UBTECH's Walker S1, so be on the lookout for more industry partnerships and innovation.

data.title: IonQ, Ansys tout quantum edge over classical computing in medical device design
data.description:
IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) shares popped 2% Thursday morning after the company, along with partner Ansys (NASDAQ:ANSS), demonstrated quantum computing outperforming classical computing when designing key life-saving medical devices.

Ansys leveraged IonQ’s quantum computing tech to simulate blood pump dynamics on LS-DYNA simulation applications, optimizing design and enhancing efficiency by analyzing fluid interactions within medical devices. Testing showed that running the application on IonQ’s quantum systems improved processing performance by up to 12% over classical computing.

“This demonstration is a significant achievement for IonQ and the quantum computing industry as a whole,” said Niccolo de Masi, President and CEO, IonQ. “We’re showcasing one of the first cases ever where quantum computing is outperforming key classical methods, demonstrating real-world improvements for practical applications that will grow as our quantum hardware advances.”

IonQ’s current generation quantum computers, IonQ Forte and IonQ Forte Enterprise, are the latest in a line of advanced systems, boasting 36 algorithmic qubits.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Plymouth Industrial expands portfolio in Atlanta, Cincinnati
data.description:
• Plymouth Industrial REIT (NYSE:PLYM) said it has acquired the second tranche of the previously announced Cincinnati small bay industrial portfolio.
  • The portfolio has four buildings totaling 240,578 square feet for $17.9 million, representing an initial estimated net operating income yield of 7.0%.
  • The company said the portfolio is 100% leased to ten tenants.
  • The firm also acquired a 263,000-square-foot warehouse facility in Cincinnati for $23.3 million, equating to an initial estimated NOI yield of 6.7%. 
  • In Atlanta, the company bought Madison International’s 98% joint venture interest in a 297,583 square-foot warehouse facility for $23.9 million, which represents an initial estimated NOI yield of 6.8%.

data.title: Eguana Technologies to conduct share consolidation
data.description:
• Eguana Technologies (OTCQB:EGTYF) said it will conduct a 10:1 share consolidation.
  • The firm expects the consolidation to up its share price, which could increase investor interest, reduce shareholder transaction costs, and improve trading liquidity.
  • After the completion of the consolidation, the company will have ~45,195,602 shares issued and outstanding.

data.title: Autolus Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.86 beats by $0.03, revenue of $10.12M misses by $7.21M
data.description:
• Autolus Therapeutics press release (NASDAQ:AUTL): FY GAAP EPS of -$0.86 beats by $0.03.

data.title: Boston Beer attracts bull call from Citi ahead of the national launch of the Sun Cruiser brand
data.description:
The Boston Beer Company (NYSE:SAM) traded higher in early action on Thursday after Citi upgraded the beer stock to a Buy rating after having it set at Neutral.

Analyst Filippo Falorni highlighted that Boston Beer's (NYSE:SAM) topline trends over the past decade have been very volatile, with peaks associated with successful innovation launches followed by troughs based on the cycling of those success stories. "Post 3 years of negative depletions (2022-24) on the cycling of the hard seltzer boom, we see the potential for a return to volume growth in 2025 with slightly improving Truly trends, normalizing Twisted Tea growth, and the Sun Cruiser contribution," he wrote.

Notably, Boston Beer (SAM) is set for the national launch for the Sun Cruiser brand later this year. The brand includes Sun Cruiser Iced Tea & Vodka, which is made with real iced tea and vodka, offering a refreshing, non-carbonated drink with 4.5% ABV, 100 calories, and 1 gram of sugar per 12 oz serving. The product includes flavors such as Classic Iced Tea, Lemonade + Iced Tea, Raspberry, and Peach. The company is also introducing a Sun Cruiser Lemonade & Vodka drink as part of its expansion strategy.

In terms of valuation, SAM was noted by Falorini to be trading below historical levels and is seen as having the potential to grow. Citi assigned a price target of $280.

Shares of Boston Beer (SAM) rose 1.6% in premarket action to $222.57 vs. the 52-week range of $215.10 to $339.77.

data.title: Darden Restaurants reports mixed Q3 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Darden Restaurants press release (NYSE:DRI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.80 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $3.16B (+6.4% Y/Y) misses by $60M.
  • Darden's Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.40 per share on the Company's outstanding common stock. The dividend is payable on May 1, 2025 to shareholders of record at the close of business on April 10, 2025.
    • Total sales of approximately $12.1 billion vs. $12.10B consensus (prior approximately $12.1 billion)
    • Same-restaurant sales growth of approximately 1.5%*
    • New restaurant openings of 50 to 55
    • Total capital spending of approximately $650 million
    • Total inflation of approximately 2.5%
    • An effective tax rate of approximately 12.5%
    • 118.3 million weighted average diluted shares outstanding
    • Adjusted diluted net earnings per share from continuing operations of $9.45 to $9.52, excluding approximately $47 million, pre-tax, of Chuy's transaction and integration related costs vs. $9.48 consensus (prior $9.40 to $9.60).

data.title: Gambling.com Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 beats by $0.11, revenue of $35.31M beats by $0.17M

• Gambling.com press release (NASDAQ:GAMB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 beats by $0.11.

data.title: OptiNose climbs after take-private deal with Paratek Pharma
data.description:
Shares of OptiNose (NASDAQ:OPTN) climbed ~61% in the premarket on Thursday after the specialty pharmaceutical company agreed to be acquired by antibiotic drugmaker Paratek Pharmaceuticals in a take-private deal worth up to $330M.

Per the deal terms, privately held Paratek will acquire OptiNose’s (NASDAQ:OPTN) outstanding shares for $9 per share in cash in addition to a contingent value right worth $5 per share, subject to certain sales milestones achieved by OPTN’s lead asset Xhance.

The upfront consideration of $9 per share implies a 50% premium to Wednesday's closing share price of Yardley, Pennsylvania-based OptiNose (NASDAQ:OPTN).

After approving Xhance in 2017 to treat chronic rhinosinusitis (CRS) with nasal polyps, the FDA broadened the drug-device combination’s label last year to include CRS patients without nasal polyps, increasing its addressable market by about ten times.

The transaction, expected to close in mid-2025, is funded by entities including Novo Holdings, the investment arm of Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) controlling shareholder, which acquired Paratek Pharmaceuticals in 2023 with B-FLEXION.
